nodes.name: Greater ground robin
nodes.summary: The greater ground robin (Amalocichla sclateriana) is a species of bird in the family Petroicidae. It is found in New Guinea. Taxonomy and systematics There are two recognised subspecies of the greater ground robin: Amalocichla sclateriana sclateriana - The nominate subspecies, found in the mountains of the Huon Peninsula. Amalocichla sclateriana occidentalis - Found in the Oranje Mountains in central New Guinea. It has browner and more mottled underparts than the nominate subspecies does. Description It is the largest robin in New Guinea, at 20 cm (7.9 in) in length. Distribution and habitat It is endemic to New Guinea. It is found at elevations of 2,500–3,900 m (8,200–12,800 ft). Behaviour and ecology Diet It feeds on invertebrates, such as beetles. References Cited text Pratt, Thane K. & Beehler, Bruce N. (2014). Birds of New Guinea: The Second Edition. Princeton University Press. ISBN 978-0-691-09563-9.
nodes.shortSummary: The greater ground robin (Amalocichla sclateriana) is a species of bird in the family Petroicidae. It is found in New Guinea.
